1.source: Hwæt, iċ swefna cyst     secgan wylle,
1.translation: Listen! I will speak of the sweetest dream, what came to me in the middle of the night,

2.source: hwæt mē ġemǣtte     tō midre nihte
2.translation: Listen! I will speak of the sweetest dream, what came to me in the middle of the night, when speech-bearers slept in their rest.

3.translation: what came to me in the middle of the night, when speech-bearers slept in their rest.  It seemed that I saw a most wondrous tree

4.source: Þūhte mē þæt iċ ġesāwe     syllicre trēow
4.translation: when speech-bearers slept in their rest.  It seemed that I saw a most wondrous tree raised on high, wound round with light,

5.translation: It seemed that I saw a most wondrous tree raised on high, wound round with light, the brightest of beams. All that beacon was

6.source: bēama beorhtost.     Eall þæt bēacen wæs
6.translation: raised on high, wound round with light, the brightest of beams. All that beacon was covered in gold; gems stood

7.translation: the brightest of beams. All that beacon was covered in gold; gems stood fair at the earth’s corners, and there were five

8.source: fæġere æt foldan scēatum;     swylċe þǣr fīfe wǣron
8.translation: covered in gold; gems stood fair at the earth’s corners, and there were five up on the cross-beam. All the angels of the Lord looked on;

9.source: uppe on þām eaxleġespanne.     Behēoldon þǣr enġel Dryhtnes ealle
9.translation: fair at the earth’s corners, and there were five up on the cross-beam. All the angels of the Lord looked on; fair through all eternity; that was no felon’s gallows,

10.source: fæġere þurh forðġesceaft.     Ne wæs ðǣr hūru fracodes ġealga,
10.translation: up on the cross-beam. All the angels of the Lord looked on; fair through all eternity; that was no felon’s gallows, but holy spirits beheld him there,

11.source: ac hine þǣr behēoldon     hāliġe gāstas,
11.translation: fair through all eternity; that was no felon’s gallows, but holy spirits beheld him there, men over the earth and all this glorious creation.

12.source: men ofer moldan,     ond eall þēos mǣre ġesceaft.
12.translation: but holy spirits beheld him there, men over the earth and all this glorious creation.      Wondrous was the victory-tree, and I was stained by sins,

13.source: Sylliċ wæs se siġebēam     ond iċ synnum fāh,
13.translation: men over the earth and all this glorious creation.      Wondrous was the victory-tree, and I was stained by sins, wounded with guilt; I saw the tree of glory

14.source: forwunded mid wommum.     Ġeseah iċ wuldres trēow
14.translation: Wondrous was the victory-tree, and I was stained by sins, wounded with guilt; I saw the tree of glory honored in garments, shining with joys,

15.translation: wounded with guilt; I saw the tree of glory honored in garments, shining with joys, bedecked with gold; gems had

16.translation: honored in garments, shining with joys, bedecked with gold; gems had covered worthily the Creator’s tree.

17.translation: bedecked with gold; gems had covered worthily the Creator’s tree. And yet beneath that gold I began to see

18.source: Hwæðre iċ þurh þæt gold     onġytan meahte
18.translation: covered worthily the Creator’s tree. And yet beneath that gold I began to see an ancient wretched struggle, when it first began

19.source: earmra ǣrġewin,     þæt hit ǣrest ongan
19.translation: And yet beneath that gold I began to see an ancient wretched struggle, when it first began to bleed on the right side. I was all beset with sorrows,

20.source: swǣtan on þā swīðran healfe.     Eall iċ wæs mid sorgum ġedrēfed;
20.translation: an ancient wretched struggle, when it first began to bleed on the right side. I was all beset with sorrows, fearful for that fair vision; I saw that eager beacon

21.source: forht iċ wæs for þǣre fæġran ġesyhðe.     Ġeseah iċ þæt fūse bēacen
21.translation: to bleed on the right side. I was all beset with sorrows, fearful for that fair vision; I saw that eager beacon change garments and colors––now it was drenched,

22.source: wendan wǣdum ond blēom;     hwīlum hit wæs mid wǣtan bestēmed,
22.translation: fearful for that fair vision; I saw that eager beacon change garments and colors––now it was drenched, stained with blood, now bedecked with treasure.

23.source: beswyled mid swātes gange,     hwīlum mid since ġeġyrwed.
23.translation: change garments and colors––now it was drenched, stained with blood, now bedecked with treasure. And yet, lying there a long while,

24.source: Hwæðre iċ þǣr licgende     lange hwīle
24.translation: stained with blood, now bedecked with treasure. And yet, lying there a long while, I beheld in sorrow the Savior’s tree

25.translation: And yet, lying there a long while, I beheld in sorrow the Savior’s tree until I heard it utter a sound;

26.source: oð ðæt iċ ġehȳrde     þæt hit hlēoðrode.
26.translation: I beheld in sorrow the Savior’s tree until I heard it utter a sound; that best of woods began to speak words:

27.source: Ongan þā word sprecan     wudu sēlesta:
27.translation: until I heard it utter a sound; that best of woods began to speak words: “It was so long ago––I remember it still––

28.source: Þæt wæs ġeāra iū     (iċ þæt ġȳta ġeman)
28.translation: that best of woods began to speak words: “It was so long ago––I remember it still–– that I was felled from the forest’s edge,

29.source: þæt iċ wæs āhēawen     holtes on ende,
29.translation: “It was so long ago––I remember it still–– that I was felled from the forest’s edge, ripped up from my roots. Strong enemies seized me there,

30.source: āstyred of stefne mīnum.     Ġenāman mē ðǣr strange fēondas,
30.translation: that I was felled from the forest’s edge, ripped up from my roots. Strong enemies seized me there, made me their spectacle, made me bear their criminals;

31.source: ġeworhton him þǣr tō wǣfersȳne,     hēton mē heora wergas hebban.
31.translation: ripped up from my roots. Strong enemies seized me there, made me their spectacle, made me bear their criminals; they bore me on their shoulders and then set me on a hill,

32.source: Bǣron mē ðǣr beornas on eaxlum     oð ðæt hīe mē on beorg āsetton;
32.translation: made me their spectacle, made me bear their criminals; they bore me on their shoulders and then set me on a hill, enemies enough fixed me fast. Then I saw the Lord of mankind

33.source: ġefæstnodon mē þǣr fēondas ġenōge.     Ġeseah iċ þā Frēan mancynnes
33.translation: they bore me on their shoulders and then set me on a hill, enemies enough fixed me fast. Then I saw the Lord of mankind hasten eagerly, when he wanted to ascend upon me.

34.source: efstan elne myċle     þæt hē mē wolde on ġestīgan.
34.translation: enemies enough fixed me fast. Then I saw the Lord of mankind hasten eagerly, when he wanted to ascend upon me. I did not dare to break or bow down

35.source: Þǣr iċ þā ne dorste     ofer Dryhtnes word
35.translation: hasten eagerly, when he wanted to ascend upon me. I did not dare to break or bow down against the Lord’s word, when I saw

36.source: būgan oððe berstan,     þā iċ bifian ġeseah
36.translation: I did not dare to break or bow down against the Lord’s word, when I saw the ends of the earth tremble. Easily I might

37.translation: against the Lord’s word, when I saw the ends of the earth tremble. Easily I might have felled all those enemies, and yet I stood fast.

38.source: fēondas ġefyllan,     hwæðre iċ fæste stōd.
38.translation: the ends of the earth tremble. Easily I might have felled all those enemies, and yet I stood fast. Then the young hero made ready—that was God almighty—

39.source: Onġyrede hine þā ġeong hæleð     - þæt wæs God ælmihtiġ,
39.translation: have felled all those enemies, and yet I stood fast. Then the young hero made ready—that was God almighty— strong and resolute; he ascended on the high gallows,

40.source: strang ond stīðmōd.     Ġestāh hē on ġealgan hēanne,
40.translation: Then the young hero made ready—that was God almighty— strong and resolute; he ascended on the high gallows, brave in the sight of many, when he wanted to ransom mankind.

41.source: mōdiġ on maniġra ġesyhðe,     þā hē wolde mancyn lȳsan.
41.translation: strong and resolute; he ascended on the high gallows, brave in the sight of many, when he wanted to ransom mankind. I trembled when he embraced me, but I dared not bow to the ground,

42.source: Bifode iċ þā mē se beorn ymbclypte.     Ne dorste iċ hwæðre būgan tō eorðan,
42.translation: brave in the sight of many, when he wanted to ransom mankind. I trembled when he embraced me, but I dared not bow to the ground, or fall to the earth’s corners––I had to stand fast.

43.source: feallan tō foldan scēatum,     ac iċ sceolde fæste standan.
43.translation: I trembled when he embraced me, but I dared not bow to the ground, or fall to the earth’s corners––I had to stand fast. I was reared as a cross: I raised up the mighty King,

44.source: Rōd wæs iċ ārǣred.     Āhōf iċ rīċne Cyning,
44.translation: or fall to the earth’s corners––I had to stand fast. I was reared as a cross: I raised up the mighty King, the Lord of heaven; I dared not lie down.

45.source: heofona Hlāford,     hyldan mē ne dorste.
45.translation: I was reared as a cross: I raised up the mighty King, the Lord of heaven; I dared not lie down. They drove dark nails through me; the scars are still visible,

46.source: Þurhdrifan hī mē mid deorcan næġlum.     On mē syndon þā dolg ġesīene
46.translation: the Lord of heaven; I dared not lie down. They drove dark nails through me; the scars are still visible, open wounds of hate; I dared not harm any of them.

47.source: opene inwidhlemmas.     Ne dorste iċ hira nǣnigum sceððan.
47.translation: They drove dark nails through me; the scars are still visible, open wounds of hate; I dared not harm any of them. They mocked us both together; I was all drenched with blood

48.source: Bysmeredon hīe unc būtū ætgædere.     Eall iċ wæs mid blōde bestēmed,
48.translation: open wounds of hate; I dared not harm any of them. They mocked us both together; I was all drenched with blood flowing from that man’s side after he had sent forth his spirit.

49.source: begoten of þæs guman sīdan     siððan hē hæfde his gāst onsended.
49.translation: They mocked us both together; I was all drenched with blood flowing from that man’s side after he had sent forth his spirit.       “Much have I endured on that hill

50.source: Feala iċ on þām beorge     ġebiden hæbbe
50.translation: flowing from that man’s side after he had sent forth his spirit.       “Much have I endured on that hill of hostile fates: I saw the God of hosts

51.source: wrāðra wyrda.     Ġeseah iċ weruda God
51.translation: “Much have I endured on that hill of hostile fates: I saw the God of hosts cruelly stretched out. Darkness had covered

52.translation: of hostile fates: I saw the God of hosts cruelly stretched out. Darkness had covered with its clouds the Ruler’s corpse,

53.translation: cruelly stretched out. Darkness had covered with its clouds the Ruler’s corpse, that shining radiance. Shadows spread

54.translation: with its clouds the Ruler’s corpse, that shining radiance. Shadows spread grey under the clouds; all creation wept,

55.source: wann under wolcnum.     Wēop eal ġesceaft,
55.translation: that shining radiance. Shadows spread grey under the clouds; all creation wept, mourned the King’s fall: Christ on the cross.

56.source: cwīðdon Cyninges fyll.     Crist wæs on rōde.
56.translation: grey under the clouds; all creation wept, mourned the King’s fall: Christ on the cross. And yet from afar men came hastening

57.translation: mourned the King’s fall: Christ on the cross. And yet from afar men came hastening to that noble one; I watched it all.

58.source: tō þām æðelinge;     iċ þæt eall behēold.
58.translation: And yet from afar men came hastening to that noble one; I watched it all. I was all beset with sorrow, yet I sank into their hands,

59.source: Sāre iċ wæs mid sorgum ġedrēfed;     hnāg iċ hwæðre þām secgum tō handa,
59.translation: to that noble one; I watched it all. I was all beset with sorrow, yet I sank into their hands, humbly, eagerly. There they took almighty God,

60.source: ēaðmōd, elne myċle.     Ġenāmon hīe þǣr ælmihtiġne God,
60.translation: I was all beset with sorrow, yet I sank into their hands, humbly, eagerly. There they took almighty God, lifted him from his heavy torment; the warriors then left me

61.source: āhōfon hine of ðām hefian wīte.     Forlēton mē þā hilderincas
61.translation: humbly, eagerly. There they took almighty God, lifted him from his heavy torment; the warriors then left me standing drenched in blood, all shot through with arrows.

62.source: standan stēame bedrifenne.     Eall iċ wæs mid strǣlum forwundod.
62.translation: lifted him from his heavy torment; the warriors then left me standing drenched in blood, all shot through with arrows. They laid him down, bone-weary, and stood by his body’s head;

63.source: Ālēdon hīe ðǣr limwēriġne,     ġestōdon him æt his līċes hēafdum;
63.translation: standing drenched in blood, all shot through with arrows. They laid him down, bone-weary, and stood by his body’s head; they watched the Lord of heaven there, who rested a while,

64.source: behēoldon hīe ðǣr heofenes Dryhten,     ond hē hine ðǣr hwīle reste,
64.translation: They laid him down, bone-weary, and stood by his body’s head; they watched the Lord of heaven there, who rested a while, weary from his mighty battle. They began to build a tomb for him

65.source: mēðe æfter ðām miċlan ġewinne.     Ongunnon him þā moldern wyrċan
65.translation: they watched the Lord of heaven there, who rested a while, weary from his mighty battle. They began to build a tomb for him in the sight of his slayer; they carved it from bright stone,

66.source: beornas on banan ġesyhðe.     Curfon hīe ðæt of beorhtan stāne;
66.translation: weary from his mighty battle. They began to build a tomb for him in the sight of his slayer; they carved it from bright stone, and set within the Lord of victories. They began to sing a dirge for him,

67.source: ġesetton hīe ðǣron sigora Wealdend.     Ongunnon him þā sorhlēoð galan
67.translation: in the sight of his slayer; they carved it from bright stone, and set within the Lord of victories. They began to sing a dirge for him, wretched at evening, when they wished to travel hence,

68.source: earme on þā ǣfentīde.     Þā hīe woldon eft sīðian
68.translation: and set within the Lord of victories. They began to sing a dirge for him, wretched at evening, when they wished to travel hence, weary, from the glorious Lord––he rested there with little company.

69.source: mēðe fram þām mǣran þēodne;     reste hē ðǣr mǣte weorode.
69.translation: wretched at evening, when they wished to travel hence, weary, from the glorious Lord––he rested there with little company. And as we stood there, weeping, a long while

70.source: Hwæðere wē ðǣr grēotende     gōde hwīle
70.translation: weary, from the glorious Lord––he rested there with little company. And as we stood there, weeping, a long while fixed in our station, the song ascended

71.source: stōdon on staðole     syððan stefn ūp ġewāt
71.translation: And as we stood there, weeping, a long while fixed in our station, the song ascended from those warriors. The corpse grew cold,

72.translation: fixed in our station, the song ascended from those warriors. The corpse grew cold, the fair life-house. Then they began to fell us

73.source: fæġer feorgbold.     Þā ūs man fyllan ongan
73.translation: from those warriors. The corpse grew cold, the fair life-house. Then they began to fell us all to the earth––a terrible fate!

74.source: ealle tō eorðan.     Þæt wæs eġesliċ wyrd!
74.translation: the fair life-house. Then they began to fell us all to the earth––a terrible fate! They dug for us a deep pit, yet the Lord’s thanes,

75.source: Bedealf ūs man on dēopan sēaþe;     hwæðre mē þǣr Dryhtnes þeġnas,
75.translation: all to the earth––a terrible fate! They dug for us a deep pit, yet the Lord’s thanes, friends found me there…

76.translation: They dug for us a deep pit, yet the Lord’s thanes, friends found me there…  adorned me with gold and silver.

77.translation: friends found me there…  adorned me with gold and silver. “Now you can hear, my dear hero,

78.source: Nū ðū miht ġehȳran,     hæleð mīn se lēofa,
78.translation: adorned me with gold and silver. “Now you can hear, my dear hero, that I have endured the work of evil-doers,

79.source: þæt iċ bealuwara weorc     ġebiden hæbbe,
79.translation: “Now you can hear, my dear hero, that I have endured the work of evil-doers, harsh sorrows. Now the time has come

80.source: sārra sorga.     Is nū sǣl cumen
80.translation: that I have endured the work of evil-doers, harsh sorrows. Now the time has come that far and wide they will honor me,

81.source: þæt mē weorðiað     wīde ond sīde
81.translation: harsh sorrows. Now the time has come that far and wide they will honor me, men over the earth and all this glorious creation,

82.source: menn ofer moldan     ond eall þēos mǣre ġesceaft,
82.translation: that far and wide they will honor me, men over the earth and all this glorious creation, and pray to this sign. On me the Son of God

83.source: ġebiddaþ him tō þyssum bēacne.     On mē bearn Godes
83.translation: men over the earth and all this glorious creation, and pray to this sign. On me the Son of God suffered for a time; and so, glorious now

84.source: þrōwode hwīle;     for þan iċ þrymfæst nū
84.translation: and pray to this sign. On me the Son of God suffered for a time; and so, glorious now I rise up under the heavens, and am able to heal

85.source: hlīfiġe under heofenum,     ond iċ hǣlan mæġ
85.translation: suffered for a time; and so, glorious now I rise up under the heavens, and am able to heal each of those who is in awe of me.

86.source: ǣġhwylċne ānra     þāra þe him bið eġesa tō mē.
86.translation: I rise up under the heavens, and am able to heal each of those who is in awe of me. Once I was made into the worst of torments,

87.source: Iū iċ wæs ġeworden     wīta heardost,
87.translation: each of those who is in awe of me. Once I was made into the worst of torments, most hateful to all people, before I opened

88.source: lēodum lāðost,     ǣr þan iċ him līfes weġ
88.translation: Once I was made into the worst of torments, most hateful to all people, before I opened the true way of life for speech-bearers.

89.translation: most hateful to all people, before I opened the true way of life for speech-bearers.  Lo! the King of glory, Guardian of heaven’s kingdom

90.source: Hwæt, mē þā ġeweorðode     wuldres Ealdor
90.translation: the true way of life for speech-bearers.  Lo! the King of glory, Guardian of heaven’s kingdom honored me over all the trees of the forest,

91.translation: Lo! the King of glory, Guardian of heaven’s kingdom honored me over all the trees of the forest, just as he has also, almighty God, honored

92.source: swylċe swā hē his mōdor ēac,     Marian sylfe,
92.translation: honored me over all the trees of the forest, just as he has also, almighty God, honored his mother, Mary herself,

93.translation: just as he has also, almighty God, honored his mother, Mary herself, above all womankind for the sake of all men.

94.translation: his mother, Mary herself, above all womankind for the sake of all men.      “Now I bid you, my beloved hero,

95.source: Nū iċ þē hāte,     hæleð mīn se lēofa,
95.translation: above all womankind for the sake of all men.      “Now I bid you, my beloved hero, that you reveal this vision to men,

96.source: þæt ðū þās ġesyhðe     secge mannum,
96.translation: “Now I bid you, my beloved hero, that you reveal this vision to men, tell them in words that it is the tree of glory

97.source: onwrēoh wordum     þæt hit is wuldres bēam
97.translation: that you reveal this vision to men, tell them in words that it is the tree of glory on which almighty God suffered

98.source: se ðe ælmihtiġ God     on þrōwode
98.translation: tell them in words that it is the tree of glory on which almighty God suffered for mankind’s many sins

99.translation: on which almighty God suffered for mankind’s many sins and Adam’s ancient deeds.

100.translation: for mankind’s many sins and Adam’s ancient deeds. Death He tasted there, yet the Lord rose again

101.source: Dēað hē þǣr byriġde;     hwæðere eft Dryhten ārās
101.translation: and Adam’s ancient deeds. Death He tasted there, yet the Lord rose again with his great might to help mankind.

102.source: mid his miċlan mihte     mannum tō helpe.
102.translation: Death He tasted there, yet the Lord rose again with his great might to help mankind. He ascended into heaven. He will come again

103.source: Hē ðā on heofenas āstāg,     hider eft fundaþ
103.translation: with his great might to help mankind. He ascended into heaven. He will come again to this middle-earth to seek mankind

104.translation: He ascended into heaven. He will come again to this middle-earth to seek mankind on doomsday, almighty God,

105.translation: to this middle-earth to seek mankind on doomsday, almighty God,  the Lord himself and his angels with him,

106.source: ælmihtiġ God     ond his enġlas mid,
106.translation: on doomsday, almighty God,  the Lord himself and his angels with him, and He will judge—He has the power of judgment—

107.source: þæt hē þonne wile dēman,     se āh dōmes ġeweald,
107.translation: the Lord himself and his angels with him, and He will judge—He has the power of judgment— each one of them as they have earned

108.source: ānra ġehwylcum     swā hē him ǣrur hēr
108.translation: and He will judge—He has the power of judgment— each one of them as they have earned beforehand here in this loaned life.

109.translation: each one of them as they have earned beforehand here in this loaned life. No one there may be unafraid

110.source: Ne mæġ þǣr ǣniġ     unforht wesan
110.translation: beforehand here in this loaned life. No one there may be unafraid at the words which the Ruler will speak:

111.source: for þām worde     þe se Wealdend cwyð.
111.translation: No one there may be unafraid at the words which the Ruler will speak: He will ask before the multitude where the man might be

112.source: Frīneð hē for þǣre mæniġe     hwǣr se man sīe,
112.translation: at the words which the Ruler will speak: He will ask before the multitude where the man might be who for the Lord’s name would taste

113.source: se ðe for Dryhtnes naman     dēaðes wolde
113.translation: He will ask before the multitude where the man might be who for the Lord’s name would taste bitter death, as He did earlier on that tree.

114.source: biteres onbyriġan,     swā hē ǣr on ðām bēame dyde.
114.translation: who for the Lord’s name would taste bitter death, as He did earlier on that tree. But they will tremble then, and little think

115.source: Ac hīe þonne forhtiað,     ond fēa þenċaþ
115.translation: bitter death, as He did earlier on that tree. But they will tremble then, and little think what they might even begin to say to Christ.

116.source: hwæt hīe tō Criste     cweðan onġinnen.
116.translation: But they will tremble then, and little think what they might even begin to say to Christ. But no one there need be very afraid

117.source: Ne þearf ðǣr þonne ǣniġ     anforht wesan
117.translation: what they might even begin to say to Christ. But no one there need be very afraid who has borne in his breast the best of beacons;

118.source: þe him ǣr in brēostum bereð     bēacna sēlest,
118.translation: But no one there need be very afraid who has borne in his breast the best of beacons; but through the cross we shall seek the kingdom,

119.source: ac ðurh ðā rōde sceal     rīċe ġesēċan
119.translation: who has borne in his breast the best of beacons; but through the cross we shall seek the kingdom, every soul from this earthly way,

120.translation: but through the cross we shall seek the kingdom, every soul from this earthly way, whoever thinks to rest with the Ruler.”

121.source: sēo þe mid Wealdende     wunian þenċeð.
121.translation: every soul from this earthly way, whoever thinks to rest with the Ruler.”      Then I prayed to the tree with a happy heart,

122.source: Ġebæd iċ mē þā tō þan bēame     blīðe mōde,
122.translation: whoever thinks to rest with the Ruler.”      Then I prayed to the tree with a happy heart, eagerly, there where I was alone

123.source: elne myċle,     þǣr iċ āna wæs
123.translation: Then I prayed to the tree with a happy heart, eagerly, there where I was alone with little company. My spirit longed to start

124.translation: eagerly, there where I was alone with little company. My spirit longed to start on the journey forth; it has felt

125.source: āfȳsed on forðweġe;     feala ealra ġebād
125.translation: with little company. My spirit longed to start on the journey forth; it has felt so much of longing. It is now my life’s hope

126.source: langunghwīla.     Is mē nū līfes hyht
126.translation: on the journey forth; it has felt so much of longing. It is now my life’s hope that I might seek the tree of victory

127.source: þæt iċ þone siġebēam     sēċan mōte
127.translation: so much of longing. It is now my life’s hope that I might seek the tree of victory alone, more often than all men

128.translation: that I might seek the tree of victory alone, more often than all men  and honor it well. I wish for that

129.source: well weorþian.     Mē is willa tō ðām
129.translation: alone, more often than all men  and honor it well. I wish for that with all my heart, and my hope of protection is

130.source: myċel on mōde,     ond mīn mundbyrd is
130.translation: and honor it well. I wish for that with all my heart, and my hope of protection is fixed on the cross. I have few wealthy friends

131.source: ġeriht tō þǣre rōde.     Nāh iċ rīċra feala
131.translation: with all my heart, and my hope of protection is fixed on the cross. I have few wealthy friends on earth; but they all have gone forth,

132.source: frēonda on foldan,     ac hīe forð heonon
132.translation: fixed on the cross. I have few wealthy friends on earth; but they all have gone forth, fled from worldly joys and sought the King of glory;

133.source: ġewiton of worulde drēamum,     sōhton him wuldres Cyning,
133.translation: on earth; but they all have gone forth, fled from worldly joys and sought the King of glory; they live now in heaven with the High Father,

134.source: lifiaþ nū on heofenum     mid hēahfædere,
134.translation: fled from worldly joys and sought the King of glory; they live now in heaven with the High Father, and dwell in glory, and each day I look forward

135.source: wuniaþ on wuldre,     ond iċ wēne mē
135.translation: they live now in heaven with the High Father, and dwell in glory, and each day I look forward to the time when the cross of the Lord,

136.source: daga ġehwylċe     hwænne mē Dryhtnes rōd
136.translation: and dwell in glory, and each day I look forward to the time when the cross of the Lord, on which I have looked while here on this earth,

137.source: þe iċ hēr on eorðan     ǣr scēawode
137.translation: to the time when the cross of the Lord, on which I have looked while here on this earth, will fetch me from this loaned life,

138.translation: on which I have looked while here on this earth, will fetch me from this loaned life, and bring me where there is great bliss,

139.source: ond mē þonne ġebringe     þǣr is blis myċel,
139.translation: will fetch me from this loaned life, and bring me where there is great bliss, joy in heaven, where the Lord’s host

140.source: drēam on heofonum,     þǣr is Dryhtnes folc
140.translation: and bring me where there is great bliss, joy in heaven, where the Lord’s host is seated at the feast, with ceaseless bliss;

141.source: ġeseted tō symle,     þǣr is singal blis,
141.translation: joy in heaven, where the Lord’s host is seated at the feast, with ceaseless bliss; and then set me where I may afterwards

142.source: ond mē þonne āsette     þǣr iċ syþþan mōt
142.translation: is seated at the feast, with ceaseless bliss; and then set me where I may afterwards dwell in glory, have a share of joy

143.source: wunian on wuldre,     well mid þām hālgum
143.translation: and then set me where I may afterwards dwell in glory, have a share of joy fully with the saints. May the Lord be my friend,

144.source: drēames brūcan.     Sī mē Dryhten frēond,
144.translation: dwell in glory, have a share of joy fully with the saints. May the Lord be my friend, He who here on earth once suffered

145.source: se ðe hēr on eorþan     ǣr þrōwode
145.translation: fully with the saints. May the Lord be my friend, He who here on earth once suffered on the hanging-tree for human sin;

146.source: on þām ġealgtrēowe     for guman synnum.
146.translation: He who here on earth once suffered on the hanging-tree for human sin; He ransomed us and gave us life,

147.source: Hē ūs onlȳsde     ond ūs līf forġeaf
147.translation: on the hanging-tree for human sin; He ransomed us and gave us life, a heavenly home. Hope was renewed

148.translation: He ransomed us and gave us life, a heavenly home. Hope was renewed with cheer and bliss for those who were burning there.

149.source: mid blēdum ond mid blisse     þām þe þǣr bryne þolodan.
149.translation: a heavenly home. Hope was renewed with cheer and bliss for those who were burning there. The Son was successful in that journey,

150.source: Se Sunu wæs sigorfæst     on þām sīðfate,
150.translation: with cheer and bliss for those who were burning there. The Son was successful in that journey, mighty and victorious, when he came with a multitude,

151.source: mihtiġ ond spēdiġ     þā hē mid maniġeo cōm,
151.translation: The Son was successful in that journey, mighty and victorious, when he came with a multitude, a great host of souls, into God’s kingdom,

152.translation: mighty and victorious, when he came with a multitude, a great host of souls, into God’s kingdom, the one Ruler almighty, the angels rejoicing

153.translation: a great host of souls, into God’s kingdom, the one Ruler almighty, the angels rejoicing and all the saints already in heaven

154.source: ond eallum ðām hālgum     þām þe on heofonum ǣr
154.translation: the one Ruler almighty, the angels rejoicing and all the saints already in heaven dwelling in glory, when almighty God,

155.source: wunedon on wuldre     þā heora Wealdend cwōm,
155.translation: and all the saints already in heaven dwelling in glory, when almighty God, their Ruler, returned to his rightful home.

156.source: ælmihtiġ God,     þǣr his ēðel wæs.
156.translation: dwelling in glory, when almighty God, their Ruler, returned to his rightful home.
